metadata.title: Why the "natural" trigger for a review-counting check is the one that lets the author bypass it
metadata.topic: GitHub event type → which branch supplies the workflow file
metadata.purpose: The trigger event decides which ref provides the workflow file; author-controlled refs must never supply the file that judges the author. Review approvals are instead counted indirectly the next time a safe-ref event fires.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.prerequisites: GitHub Actions ref selection, `pull_request` vs `pull_request_target`, merge ref
metadata.exclusions: what the count does once computed; CODEOWNERS/branch-protection as complementary defenses
metadata.visual_object: A fork from one PR — left "review → merge ref (attacker-controlled)", right "comment → base ref (locked)"; the workflow-file icon switches sides.
metadata.hook: The approval check deliberately never triggers on `pull_request_review`, even though reviews are exactly what it counts.

beats.narration_text: A PR author edits the workflow to `required_approvals: 0`. Under `pull_request_review`, GitHub runs the attacker's modified file from the merge ref and the gate passes with zero approvals; under `issue_comment`/`pull_request_target` it runs the base-branch file the author can't touch.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: `pull_request_review` is the obvious trigger for a check that counts reviews — so why does using it hand the gated author control of the gate?
The approval check deliberately never triggers on `pull_request_review`, even though reviews are exactly what it counts.

The trigger event decides which ref provides the workflow file; author-controlled refs must never supply the file that judges the author. Review approvals are instead counted indirectly the next time a safe-ref event fires.
beats.visual: A fork from one PR — left "review → merge ref (attacker-controlled)", right "comment → base ref (locked)"; the workflow-file icon switches sides.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: PR#88 sets `required_approvals: 0`. A: `pull_request_review` → attacker's file → passes at 0. B: `/approve` comment → base file with `required_approvals: 2` → blocks.

The trigger event decides which ref provides the workflow file; author-controlled refs must never supply the file that judges the author. Review approvals are instead counted indirectly the next time a safe-ref event fires.
